summary: Small naming cleanup that replaced ambiguous `HSD_GObj* fp` parameter names with role-specific names in Game & Watch item hitlag prototypes and an item callback signature. Fighter-object parameters were renamed to `fighter_gobj` in ftGameWatch source prototypes and to `fighter` in `ItemLogicTable::x38_callback_OnUnknown`, clarifying that the second GObj is a fighter rather than an item or Fighter struct pointer.
what_changed: Renamed `ftGameWatch_ItemRescueEnterHitlag(HSD_GObj* fp)` to use `HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj` in `ftGameWatch_SpecialHi.c`.
Renamed `ftGameWatch_ItemRescueExitHitlag(HSD_GObj* fp)` to use `HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj` in `ftGameWatch_SpecialHi.c`.
Renamed `ftGameWatch_ItemJudgementExitHitlag(HSD_GObj* fp)` to use `HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj` in `ftGameWatch_SpecialS.c`.
Renamed `ftGameWatch_ItemJudgementEnterHitlag(HSD_GObj* fp)` to use `HSD_GObj* fighter_gobj` in `ftGameWatch_SpecialS.c`.
Renamed the second parameter of `ItemLogicTable::x38_callback_OnUnknown` from `HSD_GObj* fp` to `HSD_GObj* fighter` in `src/melee/it/item.h`.
No functional logic changes are evident from the diff; the PR changed parameter names only, plus unchanged end-of-file context.
smart_moves: Removed the misleading name `fp` from `HSD_GObj*` parameters. In this codebase `fp` often suggests a `Fighter*`, so using it for an `HSD_GObj*` can confuse call-site and callback reasoning.
Used `fighter_gobj` in ftGameWatch prototypes where the exact type is an `HSD_GObj*` wrapping a fighter, preserving the distinction between a fighter GObj and fighter data.
Adjusted the shared `ItemLogicTable` callback parameter name to reflect that the paired object in the item callback is a fighter-side object, not another generic pointer.
decomp_lessons: For `HSD_GObj*` parameters, prefer names that encode the object role, such as `fighter_gobj` or `item_gobj`, instead of generic or overloaded names like `fp`.
`fp` should be avoided for GObj parameters when it could be read as `Fighter*`; clearer naming reduces mistakes during struct access reconstruction and callback signature propagation.

Header callback typedefs and struct function-pointer fields are part of the decomp knowledge surface: parameter names there should be as clear as source definitions because they guide future matching work.
Even non-functional parameter-name cleanup can matter in decomp projects because it improves semantic consistency across fighter and item systems.

naming_conventions.meaning: Use for a GObj whose payload/context is a fighter object.
Use for a GObj whose payload/context is an item object; already visible nearby in `item.h` as `func_80279BE0(HSD_GObj* item_gobj)`.
`fp` is ambiguous and can imply a Fighter pointer rather than a generic object pointer.
For item callbacks with both item and fighter participants, name parameters by role, e.g. `item` and `fighter`, not generic abbreviations.

assembly_or_matching_tactics: No assembly or matching tactic was discussed in the PR context.
The diff is naming-only, so it should not affect code generation or matching.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the provided PR context.
follow_up_queries: Find remaining `HSD_GObj* fp` parameters and decide whether they should be `fighter_gobj`, `item_gobj`, or another role-specific name.
Audit item callback signatures that take both item and fighter GObjs for consistent role naming.
Check whether definitions of the Game & Watch hitlag functions use the same `fighter_gobj` naming as these prototypes.
